page: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor features a 48 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

The instance includes 154 GB of local SSD storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. No public IPv4 addresses are bundled by default, and there are no dedicated GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1639.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1896.58. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 3502.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression result categorized as poor. Memory read bandwidth is weak at 29.78 GB/sec for large blocks, though write bandwidth remains average at 25.32 GB/sec. The instance demonstrates average throughput for static web serving at 6.57 GB/sec and average text generation speeds of 110.35 tokens/sec for small LLM models, but shows weaker performance in database operations and Redis SET workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4ds_v5 offers a balanced resource profile with bundled local storage, making it a viable option for workloads that require a mix of compute, memory, and temporary storage without demanding extreme multi-core throughput. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, moderate web serving, light text processing, and development or testing environments that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and local scratch space.
description: The Microsoft Azure D4ds_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 154 GB of local SSD storage. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks reveal average single-core CPU performance and static web serving throughput, though multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak. Database operations and Redis SET performance also fall into lower tiers. Qualitatively, the D4ds_v5 provides a balanced hardware profile with integrated local storage, making it cost-efficient for workloads requiring moderate compute and memory density. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, light text processing, and development environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and 154 GB of local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor on the Hyper-V hypervisor.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ds_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose VM with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage, running on Intel Xeon 8370C processors.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and integrated local SSD storage for everyday workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM.
Includes 154 GB of local SSD storage and supports Premium Storage.
Delivers average single-core CPU performance and static web serving throughput.
Exhibits weaker multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth in benchmarks.
Best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and development environments.
